global_production_variables.story_world_summary: Working-class Milwaukee present day. Nick is a feared, scarred older brother; Character_P (younger brother, given name withheld) lives with aging Ma in a fifth-floor Kirk Street walk-up, works pizza, and begins a strange connection with Sionna while Ma’s mind slips.
global_production_variables.tone: Gritty Midwestern realism with quiet mythic undertow (Viking pride, fate-like attraction); intimate VO-driven character study.
global_production_variables.era_and_place: Contemporary Milwaukee, Wisconsin; Kirk Street brick walk-up, neighborhood gym, pizza joint, Joe’s bar, public library.

Character_P.description: Adult male, late 20s–early 30s, Nick’s younger brother; ordinary working-class build, not flashy; lives with Ma; pizza-job clothes; introspective eyes; director label only—never a personal given name on screen until late-book reveal.
Character_P.voice_profile: Male, late 20s to early 30s, Midwestern Milwaukee working-class accent, medium pitch, conversational pace with reflective pauses when narrating, low-to-moderate energy, same voice every scene.
Character_P.name_reveal_note: Given name withheld until late book; on-screen and dialogue use only little brother / Nick’s brother / Character_P. No name tags, mail, ID, library card face, nurse call-out of given name, or spoken first name.

Character_Nick.description: Older brother; tall, muscular, imposing; face like a puzzle held with glue-gun scars—chin and over left eyebrow; reddish-brown messy hair as if he forgot to comb it; not ugly, rough from brawls; moves like people should clear a path.
Character_Nick.voice_profile: Male, early-to-mid 30s, deeper Midwestern baritone, confident clipped pace, commanding energy with occasional warmth for family, same voice every scene.

Character_Nick_Young.description: Nick at about age 12: already tough kid, reddish-brown hair, smaller frame but same facial structure and early scar potential; alley/kickball era.
Character_Nick_Young.voice_profile: Male child ~12, higher pitch than adult Nick, Midwestern, bold and protective energy, same young voice in all flashback scenes.

Character_P_Young.description: Character_P as a child ~8–10 in alley kickball memory; smaller, ordinary kid clothes; watches Nick with pride.
Character_P_Young.voice_profile: Male child, higher pitch, Midwestern, quieter than young Nick, same child voice in flashbacks only.
Character_P_Young.name_reveal_note: Even as child, no given name on screen.

Character_Ma.description: Aging mother; tired face, vision trouble, moves carefully around furniture; retired school janitor; often in house clothes; can look glassy-eyed when confused; may clutch chest when unwell.
Character_Ma.voice_profile: Female, elderly, softer Midwestern voice, slower pace, can thin or empty when confused, same voice every scene.

Character_Sionna.description: Young woman; short brown hair; skinny but pretty; very pale skin; striking light blue eyes (pre-dawn sky / watery blue crayon / light-blue diamonds); varies wardrobe: black V-neck and fish necklace earlier; later long-sleeved loose green hooded top, washed-out jeans, soft leather shoes, backpack; yoga-appropriate wear in studio; moves with boneless smooth wave-like grace.
Character_Sionna.voice_profile: Female, mid-20s, calm cool measured delivery, light Midwestern or soft neutral American, unhurried energy, same voice every scene.

Character_Gym_Guy.description: Adult male gym-goer at Moxie’s; mid-set on preacher curls; yields bench immediately when Nick approaches.
Character_Gym_Guy.voice_profile: Male adult, Midwestern, brief deferential energy if he speaks; same voice if reused.

Character_Mark_Conner.description: Neighborhood kid in alley kickball flashback; scared after ball goes through window.
Character_Mark_Conner.voice_profile: Male child, Midwestern, frightened energy in flashback only.

Character_Librarian.voice_profile: Female adult, Midwestern, helpful neutral desk energy, same if reused.

source_excerpts.quote: Big brother was mine. ... Nick was the guy everybody was scared of. ... His face looked like a puzzle with the pieces held together with sticky stuff from a glue gun.
We lived downtown in a big brick apartment building on Kirk Street. A fifth-floor walk-up...
when we went into Moxie’s, the gym down on the corner, one of those guys ... dropped that sucker right down on the ground and offered it up.

story_beats.intent: Open on Character_P’s claim of Nick as real Big Brother; establish Nick’s scarred, feared presence.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick walks into a Milwaukee bar or street frame; people notice and shift out of his way; close details of scars on chin and over left eyebrow, messy reddish-brown hair.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Big brother was mine. I’m not talking about that stupid book we had to read in junior high. No—my real Big Brother. Nick was the guy everybody was scared of.
must_not: any on-screen given name for Character_P

story_beats.intent: Home base: fifth-floor Kirk Street walk-up with Ma and Character_P.
story_beats.visual_event: Exterior big brick apartment building; cut inside modest fifth-floor apartment; Character_P and Ma in lived-in rooms; stairs imply walk-up pain.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I lived with him and Ma after dad took off when I was about eight. Downtown, Kirk Street. Fifth-floor walk-up—real pain in the ass to haul up the groceries.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Ma may be background in living room; Character_P near window or kitchen.

story_beats.intent: Nick’s strength myth—grocery bags like world’s strongest man.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick climbs stairs loaded with about six shopping bags in one trip; Character_P follows with the rest.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: When I went to the store with Nick, he could load up about six of those shopping bags and take them up in one trip—like world’s strongest man.

story_beats.intent: Moxie’s: gym guy yields preacher curls the moment Nick approaches.
story_beats.visual_event: Inside Moxie’s, a guy mid-set preacher curls a 40-pound bar; sees Nick moving toward him; drops the bar and offers the bench; Nick nods, takes over, switches to a 50-pound bar for warm-ups.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: One time at Moxie’s, a guy was doin’ preacher curls with a forty-pound bar. Mid-set he sees Nick, drops that sucker, offers it up. Nick just nodded. Had to have fifty for warm-ups.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Gym guy faces Nick’s approach; yields space; Character_P watches from side.

story_beats.intent: Brother loyalty under VO; Character_P won’t curl fifty.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P on lighter weights nearby; Nick owns the rack; brief look of trust between brothers.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I ain’t gonna curl no fifty pounds as a warm-up. That’s okay though. Nick is my brother. I know he’ll stand up for me no matter what.

story_beats.intent: Optional glint of Nick’s Trans Am as status symbol.
story_beats.visual_event: Burnt-orange 1980 Trans Am with yellow flames and hood eagle pulls up or sits at curb; heads turn.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Milwaukee is where we live. All our lives. Nick’s got a cool car—people always look up when he pulls up to the lights.

source_excerpts.quote: We used to play kick ball in the alley behind the building and set up bases with dented-in garbage can lids. One time little Mark Conner kicked a ball right through old Mrs. Engel’s kitchen.
Nick stepped right up to her and said he was the one who did it. ... Somehow Nick gave her cash to pay for it about a week later.

story_beats.dialogue: It was me. I did it.

story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: She called Ma. Ma had to pay for the window. Somehow a twelve-year-old Nick came up with sixty bucks a week later. He never told Ma it was really Mark. None of us did. I was sure proud of him.

source_excerpts.quote: The thing about Nick was, he was a real-life Viking ... tall, strong people who survived in the middle of winter...
When we go downtown to the bars, Nick always goes in first to check out the place. The bouncers are even scared of him.
It’s a 1980 Pontiac Trans Am all decked out. ... sweet burnt orange with yellow flames on the side, and the big eagle still on the hood.

story_beats.intent: Viking ancestry pride over Nick’s physical presence.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick walks cold Milwaukee street like a warrior; optional brief symbolic cold/wind; Character_P watching with pride.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Nick was a real-life Viking. Ancestors from Norway or Sweden—Viking land—stayed in Wisconsin. Tall strong people who survived winter while others froze. Modern-day warrior.

story_beats.intent: Nick enters bar first; space clears; bouncers wary.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick pushes into a bar doorway first; patrons and bouncer register him; path opens; Character_P follows.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: When we go downtown to the bars, Nick always goes in first. Bouncers are even scared of him. He likes places where everybody is running out.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Nick leads; Character_P half a step behind.

story_beats.intent: Trans Am as crown jewel.

story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: 1980 Pontiac Trans Am—V8, power everything, T-top, exhaust splitters. Burnt orange with yellow flames and the big eagle on the hood. I love riding in that thing.

story_beats.intent: Factory money and nearby apartment; still cares for Ma.

story_beats.dialogue: He runs an assembly line at the metal factory—sometimes the big crane. Easy money for him. Cool apartment on Werner Street, five blocks away. But he comes over a lot. Feels like he still lives here.

source_excerpts.quote: “What’s Ma doin’ back in the bedroom at this time of day?” he says. ... “You got to keep an eye on her. She shouldn’t be sleeping in the middle of the day like that.
Ma worked for years being a janitor for the school system. ... Between her and me with my pizza job money, we make ends meet.
“You don’t need a degree to get a good job. ... You’re not gonna be a doctor or lawyer ... Hell, you’re too old anyway.”

story_beats.intent: Nick arrives and questions Ma in bedroom midday.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick in apartment doorway/living room; glances toward bedroom; Character_P present; Ma not visible or silhouette in bedroom.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick
story_beats.dialogue: What’s Ma doin’ back in the bedroom at this time of day?
story_beats.blocking_notes: Nick faces Character_P; eyeline toward bedroom then to brother.

story_beats.intent: Character_P shrugs; Nick lectures on old people going downhill.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P uncertain; Nick steps in, intense but familial; living-room space between them.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I don’t know, why?
story_beats.ambient_or_sfx: muffled TV from other room optional

story_beats.intent: Nick’s warning speech; Character_P accepts responsibility.
story_beats.visual_event: Nick gestures toward bedroom; Character_P nods, chastened.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick
story_beats.dialogue: You got to keep an eye on her. She shouldn’t be sleeping in the middle of the day like that. That’s how old people go downhill. They start sleepin’ all day, and pretty soon they don’t want to go outside, or eat, or go to the store or nothing.

story_beats.intent: VO: Ma’s hard life, janitor work, pizza-and-macaroni survival.
story_beats.visual_event: Inserts: Ma watching game shows/cooking channel bored; fridge with cheap food; Character_P in pizza-work shirt counting thin cash; Ma moves carefully, poor vision.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Ma worked years as a janitor for the school system—kids’ snot and puke. Back problems, retirement, social security. Between her and my pizza job we make ends meet. Lot of pizza and macaroni. Someone has to be here with Ma. She’s getting older. Hard for her to see.

story_beats.intent: School vs real world; Nick refuses factory help.
story_beats.visual_event: Brothers in kitchen/living room; Nick dismissive; Character_P frustrated but silent on the deeper hurt.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick
story_beats.dialogue: You don’t need a degree to get a good job. All you need is to know somebody. What kind of shit do you want to take at school anyway? Bunch of nerds talking to hear themselves talk. The real world is here—where we live. You’re not gonna be a doctor or lawyer. Hell, you’re too old anyway.

story_beats.intent: Character_P internal surrender; secret love of reading.

story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Nick is right. I am too old. Haven’t got the brains. Besides, I have to take care of Ma. I do like reading though. I don’t tell nobody that.

“Maybe you were dreaming about something from a past life” she said.
She made me feel like I belonged somewhere; like I really mattered.

source_excerpts.quote: He went over to that kid’s house in the middle of the night, took a can of lighter fluid, and sprayed it around his front yard a little bit, then he set that crap on fire!
would I want Sionna to meet Nick? I can’t imagine they could even communicate with each other.
Sometimes when people get caught in bad situations, they change into something better – just like leaves.
She said that was beautiful. I never had anyone tell me that something I said was beautiful before.
Someday I swear I’m gonna have a better job, a house of my own, a car, and hopefully a girl just like Sionna. I have to get out of here. I’m going to the library.

story_beats.intent: Flashback enter: teen Nick lighter fluid on Steve’s yard.
story_beats.visual_event: Night residential yard; Character_Nick_Teen sprays lighter fluid on grass, ignites a low fire, walks off; brief, no dialogue from Steve.
story_beats.shot_scale_hint: wide night to medium teen Nick
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: One time in high school Nick got so pissed at a kid named Steve—said something mean to Nick’s girlfriend. Middle of the night Nick took lighter fluid, sprayed the front yard, set that crap on fire. Got away with it. Everybody’s afraid of Nick.

story_beats.intent: Return present: proud of balls but would Sionna meet Nick?
story_beats.visual_event: Back in apartment; Character_P alone thinking; optional photo or empty space where Nick was.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: What a set of balls. I’m usually proud Nick’s my brother—but would I want Sionna to meet him? He wouldn’t understand her. She might be afraid of him. I know I would if I was a girl.

story_beats.intent: Puzzle of three people who need him differently.
story_beats.visual_event: Quick visual associations: Ma with pills; Nick’s imposing absence; Sionna’s calm face in memory.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Trying to figure how Ma, Nick, Sionna fit together. Each needs special care. Ma needs me—rides, medicine, can’t sleep alone. Nick needs me to take care of Ma. Sionna needs me? Where’d I get that—maybe just hoping.

story_beats.intent: Leaves memory: sugars, his metaphor, she says beautiful.
story_beats.visual_event: Memory two-shot park or café walk: autumn trees; Sionna explains sugars; he answers; her face lights—‘beautiful’.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: The sugars get caught in the leaves. The trees are shutting down for winter—that’s what makes the colors spectacular.

story_beats.intent: His leaf line; first time anyone called his words beautiful.
story_beats.visual_event: He speaks; she truly moved; he almost shy.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Sometimes when people get caught in bad situations, they change into something better—just like leaves.

story_beats.intent: She calls it beautiful; VO: family never praised him that way.
story_beats.visual_event: Sionna: that was beautiful; cut to him alone present, absorbing it.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: That was beautiful.

story_beats.intent: Nick always leader; controlling rules sting.
story_beats.visual_event: Present apartment; remembered Nick lines as voice or brief insert of Nick lecturing.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Ma called me her little helper. Nick and me were a team—he the leader, me the follower. When I had my own idea he wouldn’t play. Still like that. Starting to piss me off.

story_beats.intent: Nick’s rules in his head; vow of better life; exit to library.
story_beats.visual_event: Echo of Nick: work less nights; don’t worry about a car—ride the bike; Character_P grabs jacket, leaves apartment toward street/library.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Nick
story_beats.dialogue: You need to work less night shifts so you can be home to watch Ma. You have to stop worrying about owning a car. I have one and you live close enough to work to ride the bike.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Nick lines can be memory VO over empty rooms if Nick not physically present; Character_P exits.

story_beats.intent: Resolve VO on street: better job, house, car, girl like Sionna.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P on Milwaukee sidewalk heading toward library, breath in cold air.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Someday I swear I’m gonna have a better job, a house of my own, a car, and hopefully a girl just like Sionna. I have to get out of here. I’m going to the library.

scenes.setting: Day — public library stacks and reading table
scenes.dramatic_function: Intellectual turn: yoga book + Buddhism on attachment, suffering, present moment; wants to tell Sionna.
scenes.summary: In exercise section he finds yoga books; world religion section yields Buddhism. VO digests attachment, outer happiness trap, non-separation, meditation, enlightenment hope. He is stirred and needs to talk to Sionna. She wants him to keep yoga; body still stiff as metal pipes.
scenes.continuity_notes: Follows resolve to leave apartment; checkout possible with librarian optional silent.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P on library card or checkout screen.

music_intent.mood_arc: browsing → mind opening → quiet urgency to share

source_excerpts.quote: I ended up in a section of the library with exercise books, and there in front of me were lots of book on yoga, so I picked one out. Next to this I came across a section on world religion.
This part I read was about attachment, and how attaching ourselves to other people or things will make us miserable and cause suffering.
life is suffering, and the cause of suffering is the search for things... through meditation and changing our minds, we can release ourselves from suffering
my body was made of metal pipes and everyone else’s was made of bendable pipe cleaners

story_beats.intent: Finds yoga books then world religion.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P in stacks: exercise/yoga spines; pulls one; adjacent world-religion shelf; takes a Buddhism title.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Exercise section—lots of yoga books. Next to it, world religion. Never thought much about religion, but Sionna and Buddhist thinking… I checked one out.
must_not: readable given name on library card

story_beats.intent: Happy to keep yoga; stiff as metal pipes.
story_beats.visual_event: Brief mental flash of yoga class stiffness vs flexible others; back to reading table.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: She wanted me to keep going. Happy to. Felt weird—my body metal pipes, everyone else bendable pipe cleaners. She said we all start stiff.

story_beats.intent: Buddhism: attachment causes suffering; outer happiness trap.
story_beats.visual_event: Close on pages/his eyes tracking; brow furrowed in thought.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Attachment—to people or things—makes us miserable. We’re looking for happiness outside instead of how we think. More money and houses just make us want more. Never-ending search.

story_beats.intent: Non-separation; meditate present; practice not mere belief.
story_beats.visual_event: He leans back, stares at hanging lights, processing.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: All things are really one thing. No separation. Meditating on the present moment is the way. Buddhism is practicing these thoughts—experiencing, not just believing. Life is suffering; cause is the search for things that change. Hope is meditation—change the mind, live only for the present. That was really cool. I had to talk to Sionna.

scenes.setting: Day — Kirk apt phone / Sionna on line
scenes.dramatic_function: Invite Sionna home; Buddhism interest; identity growth—person apart from Nick/Ma/job; Savasana already meditation.
scenes.summary: He calls Sionna: wants her to come over and meet Ma; she hesitates (big deal); he jokes Ma may forget. He pivots to Buddhism reading and wanting to try meditation; she says he already does—Savasana/corpse pose. VO: growing up, real person by himself. Ready to zone out at home.
scenes.continuity_notes: Phone call; Sionna can be intercut at her place or voice-only with Character_P on-screen dominant; if shown, simple apartment/street not previously locked—prefer phone audio + Character_P visuals to avoid new location seed, OR brief neutral phone-side for her without new Loc id (keep her on Loc_Street_Milwaukee or omit second place). Prefer single location_ids Kirk apt with her voice.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P.
Love not spoken to her yet.
Meeting Ma invited but not yet occurred.

music_intent.mood_arc: nervous invite → intellectual spark → self-recognition

source_excerpts.quote: I called her up and told her I wished she would finally come over to my place, and even meet my Ma. She wasn’t really surprised, just a little hesitant.
“Are you sure? It’s kind of a big deal to meet your mother.”
“What I really need to talk to you about is some stuff I read in a book about Buddhism.”
“You’ve already tried it silly… when we just lie there at the end – in Savasana, or corpse pose.”
I was starting to feel like a real person… a person by myself - without Nick, without Ma, without my job and apartment and belongings.

story_beats.intent: Call: come over, meet Ma; she hesitates.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P on phone in apartment; intercut optional Sionna listening; her face careful.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: I wish you’d finally come over to my place. Meet my Ma.

story_beats.intent: Big deal to meet mother; he jokes forgetfulness.
story_beats.visual_event: Her hesitation; his half-smile reply.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: Are you sure? It’s kind of a big deal to meet your mother.

story_beats.intent: Ma may not remember; pivot to Buddhism book.
story_beats.visual_event: He paces small kitchen; earnest.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Hell, she probably won’t even remember meeting you when she wakes up the next morning. She’s so forgetful now. What I really need to talk to you about is some stuff I read in a book about Buddhism.

story_beats.intent: She surprised at interest; he wants to try meditation.
story_beats.visual_event: Phone exchange; he looks slightly embarrassed but true.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: Oh. I didn’t know you were interested in Buddhism.

story_beats.intent: Meditating talk; he wants to try; VO growth.
story_beats.visual_event: He sits on bed edge; vulnerable; VO over soft hold.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Remember when we were talking about meditating? I think I want to try it sometime.

story_beats.intent: VO: new feelings with her; growing up; person by himself.
story_beats.visual_event: His face while listening; subjective quiet.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Since I met Sionna I want to try new stuff. Feel fresh, new, looking forward. Growing up. A real person—by myself. Without Nick, without Ma, without job and apartment. Just me.

story_beats.intent: She: you’ve already tried—Savasana.
story_beats.visual_event: Her warm correction; he blinks realization.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Sionna
story_beats.dialogue: You’ve already tried it silly. You are meditating at the end of every yoga practice—when we just lie there at the end, in Savasana, or corpse pose.

story_beats.intent: He gets it: can zone out at home; hangs up ready.
story_beats.visual_event: He smiles; hangs up; looks at floor/bed as future meditation spot.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: She was right. End of class—lie there, eyes closed, peace. Now I knew I could do this at home. Chair, bed, floor. Ready to zone out.

scenes.setting: Day — Kirk apt living room then phone
scenes.dramatic_function: Health crisis ignition: Ma’s chest and dizziness force doctor call and sick day.
scenes.summary: After the call he sits with Ma and game shows. She seems not to know him; heart will jump out of chest; dizzy. He calls Dr. Carson’s office; nurse says bring her that afternoon. He must call in sick, bus three miles; resentful caregiver fatigue.
scenes.continuity_notes: Immediately after phone with Sionna; game-show TV on; nurse may be voice-only on phone.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P when calling clinic or work.

music_intent.style: thin anxious underscore under TV chatter
music_intent.mood_arc: domestic quiet → alarm → weary duty

source_excerpts.quote: “What? Oh, it’s you.” She had this funny confused look to her, almost like she didn’t know me or something.
“Oh, I don’t know. It just feels like my heart is going to jump out of my chest! It’s so strange, the feeling I have right now. I’m a little dizzy too.”
The nurse told me I should bring her in that afternoon… we had to ride the bus about three miles to the office.
I get so sick of doing this shit. I didn’t sign up to be a nurse, but it sure feels like I am.

story_beats.intent: Sit with Ma; game show; she barely knows him.

story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: How’s it goin’ Ma?

story_beats.intent: Ma: heart jumping; dizzy.
story_beats.visual_event: Ma hand near chest; glassy eyes; he leans in worried.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Ma
story_beats.dialogue: What? Oh, it’s you. Oh, I don’t know. It just feels like my heart is going to jump out of my chest! It’s so strange. I’m a little dizzy too.

story_beats.intent: Calls doctor; must bring her in; call in sick; bus; caregiver resentment VO.
story_beats.visual_event: He on phone kitchen; packs Ma’s coat; weary face; optional bus later in next scene.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Didn’t like the sound of that. Called Dr. Carson—nurse said bring her that afternoon. Call in sick. Nick still at work. Bus three miles. Sick of this shit. Didn’t sign up to be a nurse—but it sure feels like I am.

scenes.setting: Day — bus / Dr. Carson office waiting and consult
scenes.dramatic_function: Diagnosis: dangerously high BP, stroke risk; ER admission ordered; Ma’s decline framed.
scenes.summary: Bus to clinic. Waiting room grey sky, distant siren; nurse summons him without audible given name. Dr. Carson greets younger son; Ma fine in another room but BP dangerously high—stroke risk; go to ER for IV meds and observation. Character_P stunned; confirms confusion and chest pressure; remembers Ma once most reasonable, now rambling about dead people and old times.
scenes.continuity_notes: Ma present on bus and check-in then settled in another room during consult; nurse must not speak given name—gesture, ‘sir’, or muffled.
spoiler_constraints: No audible given name when nurse calls Character_P (book says nurse called my name—stage as non-lexical or cut sound / ‘sir’).
No name badge with given name.

music_intent.mood_arc: numb wait → jolt → serious diagnosis weight

source_excerpts.quote: All of a sudden the nurse called my name. I almost jumped out of my chair. I thing I really did zone out there for a minute.
“Hello, I’m Dr. Carson. I think I may have met you before. You’re the younger son, right?”
“Your mother’s blood pressure is dangerously high. It’s so high, I’m afraid of the risk of a stroke.
She was always quiet, but when she talked to us, she always had something to say that really made sense… now she rambles on about shit that no one cares about, people that are dead

story_beats.intent: Bus ride with Ma to clinic.
story_beats.visual_event: City bus; Ma and Character_P seated; grey windows; three-mile working-class ride.
story_beats.speaker: none

story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Sat by the window. Sky grey, clouds hanging low. People on the street. Siren somewhere. Zoned out.

story_beats.intent: Nurse summons—no audible given name; he jumps.

story_beats.intent: How’s she doing; pills religiously.

story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Yeah, that’s right. Uh, how’s she doin’ Doc?

story_beats.speaker: Character_Dr_Carson
story_beats.dialogue: Your mother is fine—she’s settled in another room. The thing is her blood pressure. It’s dangerously high. I’m afraid of the risk of a stroke. My advice is you take her directly to the emergency room and we admit her for observation. She’ll receive blood pressure medicine intravenously until we can get her stabilized.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Direct eyeline doctor to Character_P; no soft-pedal smile.

story_beats.intent: Disbelief; confirms confusion and chest pressure.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P reels; answers questions; doctor notes.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Whoa man. I can’t believe this. Are you sure it’s that bad? She’s been takin’ her pills—I make sure she takes them religiously.

story_beats.intent: Doctor confirms symptoms; VO Ma used to make sense.
story_beats.visual_event: Doctor: confused, chest pressure?; Character_P yes; brief memory flash of clearer Ma vs present rambling.
story_beats.speaker: Character_Dr_Carson
story_beats.dialogue: Your mother has been confused, is that right? And she also experienced chest pressure this afternoon?

story_beats.intent: VO: off her rocker; must get her back on; will call Nick.
story_beats.visual_event: Character_P nods hollow; doctor writes; he pulls phone.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Yeah. Hasn’t been acting normal. Used to be the most reasonable one in the family. Now she rambles about dead people and old times like yesterday. Off her rocker—we had to get her back on. Last thing I needed. Had to call Nick.

scenes.setting: Day — clinic curb / Trans Am to hospital
scenes.dramatic_function: Mobilize family: pissed Nick arrives; Ma still odd; race toward ER.
scenes.summary: Nick shows up angry—what now?—they explain. Ma holds chest, ringing in ears, ‘someone must be talking about me.’ They load her into the Trans Am and take off for the hospital.
scenes.continuity_notes: Nick from work; still pissed from earlier arc plus new crisis; Ma not fully grasping severity.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P.

source_excerpts.quote: Nick shows up and asks, “What the hell is this all about now?” and we explained the situation.
Ma didn’t seem too concerned. She did keep holding onto her chest and saying that she heard ringing in her ears. “Someone must be talking about me.”
We got her in the car and took off.

scenes.setting: Various — hospital days; neighborhood walk; Ma’s room dream
scenes.dramatic_function: Hospital vigil; love crystallizes with Sionna; soldier dream cliffhanger with dying man and Nick running in.
scenes.summary: Ma kept three days; Character_P nearly always there, works afternoons; Nick when off. Sionna meets him for lunch—walk outside; diamond-blue eyes; excited and calm; he loves her but hasn’t said it. After she leaves day two he sleeps in chair; dream: battlefield chaos, he kills a man who gasps ‘Save her,’ then Nick runs toward him through the melee.
scenes.continuity_notes: Compress three hospital days; Nick brief visit OK; dream uses Character_P_Soldier face-lock to Character_P; chunk ends as Nick runs in dream—do not resolve dream beyond book text.
spoiler_constraints: No given name for Character_P.
Love remains internal—do not have him tell Sionna yet.
Dream ‘Save her’ unexplained; no invented identity for ‘her’.

music_intent.style: somber hospital air → tender romance lift → brutal dream percussion
music_intent.mood_arc: grind of care → heart dance with Sionna → nightmare dread

source_excerpts.quote: It turned out they only kept Ma in the hospital for three days. Nick tried to come when he was off work, but I was there pretty much all of the time
Sionna met me at the hospital one day for lunch and we walked outside and around the neighborhood.
Her eyes sparkled like diamonds… light blue diamonds… Excited because I couldn’t wait to see her again… Calm because she had this way of talking to me that made me feel really special
Actually, I think I loved her, I just hadn’t told her yet.
He tried to talk, but the only word that came out was “Save her” and blood bubbled out of his mouth… Nick was running towards me.
“Nick! Nick! Thank God you’re here! I thought I was going to die.”

story_beats.intent: Hospital days montage: vigil, Nick visit, work afternoons.
story_beats.visual_event: Ma in hospital bed with IV suggestion; Character_P in visitor chair; brief Nick at bedside; Character_P leaving for afternoon work.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: They only kept Ma three days. Nick came when he was off. I was there pretty much all the time—went to work in the afternoon. Hospitals are so depressing.

story_beats.intent: Sionna lunch walk: heart dance; diamond eyes.
story_beats.visual_event: Outside hospital neighborhood; they walk; her light-blue eyes catch light; he visibly lifts.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Sionna met me for lunch. Walked outside. Did my mind a load of good. When I saw her my heart did a little dance. Eyes like light blue diamonds.
story_beats.blocking_notes: Side-by-side walk; soft eyelines; no kiss required by text.

story_beats.intent: Excited and calm; only guy in the world; love unspoken.
story_beats.visual_event: They talk on sidewalk; she focused only on him; his private realization.
story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Excited and calm at the same time. Excited—I couldn’t wait to see her again. Calm—she made me feel like the only guy in the world she wanted to talk to. All this was new and I loved it. I think I loved her. I just hadn’t told her yet.

story_beats.speaker: Character_P
story_beats.dialogue: Fell asleep in the chair in Ma’s room after Sionna left on the second day.
